prompt: We're an RIA and one of our advisers just recommended a proprietary fund to a client when a cheaper third-party option with similar performance exists. The client is a retired teacher, moderate risk tolerance, $600K portfolio. Is there a fiduciary issue here?

assertions.text: Applies IA Act Section 206 fiduciary framework
Analyzes both duty of care and duty of loyalty
Identifies proprietary fund as conflict of interest
Does not apply FINRA suitability as primary standard
